items.title: United States: ICE agents patrol airports amid govt shutdown
items.standfirst: US immigration agency began deploying to major airports on Monday, as officials scrambled to ease mounting travel disruption during a prolonged, partial government shutdown. The move places Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) personnel – already under intense scrutiny after fatal shootings linked to immigration operations – in highly visible roles at crowded transport hubs across the country.
items.content: US immigration agency began deploying to major airports on Monday, as officials scrambled to ease mounting travel disruption during a prolonged, partial government shutdown. The move places Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) personnel – already under intense scrutiny after fatal shootings linked to immigration operations – in highly visible roles at crowded transport hubs across the country.

items.title: Delta suspends special service perk for Congress members amid DHS funding battle
items.standfirst: Airline CEOs have urged Congress to restore funding to the TSA as lengthy security lines plague US airportsSign up for the Breaking News US email to get newsletter alerts in your inboxDelta Air Lines is partly suspending its speciality service desk for members of Congress until funding for the Transportation Security Administration (TSA) is restored, the airline confirmed on Tuesday, as security lines stretch for hours at airports across the country due to the Department of Homeland Security (DHS) shutdown.The service desk is used to help members of Congress book flights at special government rates, secure airport escorts and make last-minute changes to flights. Continue reading...

Continue reading...

items.title: Trump meets with Republican senators over DHS funding
items.standfirst: President Trump met with Republican senators on Monday night to discuss Department of Homeland Security funding as the partial shutdown continues. Meanwhile, Mr. Trump also signaled he was open to a deal with Iran. CBS News' Taurean Small and Natalie Brand have the latest.
items.content: President Trump met with Republican senators on Monday night to discuss Department of Homeland Security funding as the partial shutdown continues. Meanwhile, Mr. Trump also signaled he was open to a deal with Iran. CBS News' Taurean Small and Natalie Brand have the latest.

items.title: TSA workers try to survive second shutdown and ICE influx: ‘We need to be paid’
items.standfirst: Workers are ‘in the middle of chaos from political games’ as Senate Republicans try to negotiate with Democrats to reopen DHSSign up for the Breaking News US email to get newsletter alerts in your inboxWorkers with the Transportation Security Administration (TSA) are reeling from the White House’s deployment of immigration law enforcement into airports as TSA workers enter their sixth week without pay as the Department of Homeland Security (DHS) shutdown continues.More than 400 TSA workers have quit since the shutdown began in February, with major US airports reporting high call-out rates among workers, leading to longer security wait times. On Sunday, more than, 3,450 TSA officers called out of work, with as many as 40% of officers at some airports calling out that day, according to DHS data. Continue reading...
items.content: Workers are ‘in the middle of chaos from political games’ as Senate Republicans try to negotiate with Democrats to reopen DHSSign up for the Breaking News US email to get newsletter alerts in your inboxWorkers with the Transportation Security Administration (TSA) are reeling from the White House’s deployment of immigration law enforcement into airports as TSA workers enter their sixth week without pay as the Department of Homeland Security (DHS) shutdown continues.More than 400 TSA workers have quit since the shutdown began in February, with major US airports reporting high call-out rates among workers, leading to longer security wait times. On Sunday, more than, 3,450 TSA officers called out of work, with as many as 40% of officers at some airports calling out that day, according to DHS data. Continue reading...

items.title: Who is the new US homeland security chief Markwayne Mullin?
items.standfirst: Following a 54 to 45 Senate vote, Oklahoma Senator Markwayne Mullin has been appointed the new Department of Homeland Security Secretary of the United States of America. He takes the helm of an agency in transition following the high-profile dismissal of Kristi Noem earlier in March.
items.content: Following a 54 to 45 Senate vote, Oklahoma Senator Markwayne Mullin has been appointed the new Department of Homeland Security Secretary of the United States of America. He takes the helm of an agency in transition following the high-profile dismissal of Kristi Noem earlier in March.

items.title: At least 66 killed after Colombian military plane crashes in southern Amazon
items.standfirst: Transport plane carrying soldiers and crew crashed shortly after takeoff from Puerto Leguízamo, deep in Colombia’s southern Amazon regionA Colombian military transport plane with 121 people on board, mostly soldiers, crashed shortly after takeoff in the country’s south, killing at least 66 people, authorities said.The defence minister, Pedro Sánchez, said the accident happened as the Lockheed Martin Hercules C-130 plane was taking off from Puerto Leguízamo, deep in Colombia’s southern Amazon region, on the border with Peru, as it transported troops from the armed forces. Continue reading...

items.title: ICE agents deploy to major US airports as security queues stretch for hours
items.standfirst: Hundreds of ICE agents have been deployed to 14 airports, in cities including New York, Atlanta and Houston.
items.content: Hundreds of ICE agents have been deployed to 14 airports, in cities including New York, Atlanta and Houston.

items.title: ICE Invades Airports Across the US
items.standfirst: Agents from ICE are being deployed to over a dozen airports around the country, including New York, Atlanta, and Chicago.
items.content: Agents from ICE are being deployed to over a dozen airports around the country, including New York, Atlanta, and Chicago.

clusters.title: Oil falls and shares rebound after Trump says talks have been held to end war
clusters.neutral_headline: Oil falls and shares rebound after Trump says talks have been held to end war
bullet_summary: Reported by BBC World, Guardian Business

items.title: Oil falls and shares rebound after Trump says talks have been held to end war
items.standfirst: Energy prices fall and stock markets rebound after the US president says "very good and productive" talks have been held.
items.content: Energy prices fall and stock markets rebound after the US president says "very good and productive" talks have been held.

items.title: Stock markets swing and oil prices fall after Trump postpones strikes on Iran power plants
items.standfirst: European shares start to rise after US president says talks have been ‘very good and productive’Business live – latest updatesMiddle East crisis – live updatesGlobal stock markets swung wildly and oil prices fell on Monday after Donald Trump postponed US attacks on Iranian power plants for five days.European stock markets, which had been falling sharply in the hours before Trump’s social media post, mostly rose on Monday as relieved investors digested the update. Continue reading...
items.content: European shares start to rise after US president says talks have been ‘very good and productive’Business live – latest updatesMiddle East crisis – live updatesGlobal stock markets swung wildly and oil prices fell on Monday after Donald Trump postponed US attacks on Iranian power plants for five days.European stock markets, which had been falling sharply in the hours before Trump’s social media post, mostly rose on Monday as relieved investors digested the update. Continue reading...

clusters.title: Israeli settlers attack West Bank villages
clusters.neutral_headline: Israeli settlers attack West Bank villages
bullet_summary: Reported by NBC World, Sky News World

items.title: Israeli settlers attack West Bank villages
items.standfirst: Several Palestinians were wounded in the wave of attacks, which came during Eid al-Fitr, the holiday marking the end of Ramadan.
items.content: Several Palestinians were wounded in the wave of attacks, which came during Eid al-Fitr, the holiday marking the end of Ramadan.

items.title: Cars and homes burnt as Israeli settlers rampage through occupied West Bank
items.standfirst: Israeli settlers have injured at least 10 Palestinians, as well as smashing cars and setting fires, as they rampaged through villages in the West Bank.
items.content: Israeli settlers have injured at least 10 Palestinians, as well as smashing cars and setting fires, as they rampaged through villages in the West Bank.

clusters.title: 'Every indicator is flashing red,' says UN as it warns of record 'climate imbalance'
clusters.neutral_headline: UN warns Earth's climate being 'pushed beyond its limits' as El Niño looms
bullet_summary: Reported by Sky News World, BBC World

items.title: 'Every indicator is flashing red,' says UN as it warns of record 'climate imbalance'
items.standfirst: The Earth's climate is in a "state of emergency", according to the United Nations which has warned it is more out of balance than at any other time in observed history.
items.content: The Earth's climate is in a "state of emergency", according to the United Nations which has warned it is more out of balance than at any other time in observed history.

items.title: UN warns Earth's climate being 'pushed beyond its limits' as El Niño looms
items.standfirst: The Earth's climate is further out of balance than at any time in recorded history, the UN's weather agency says.
items.content: The Earth's climate is further out of balance than at any time in recorded history, the UN's weather agency says.
